spans.description:
SELECT chatbots.id 
FROM chatbots 
WHERE chatbots.organization_id = $1::VARCHAR AND chatbots.chatbot_id = $2::VARCHAR AND chatbots.is_active IS true AND ((EXISTS (SELECT 1 
FROM users, user_chatbots 
WHERE chatbots.id = user_chatbots.chatbot_id AND users.id = user_chatbots.user_id AND users.username = $3::VARCHAR)) OR (EXISTS (SELECT 1 
FROM user_domains, user_domain_chatbots 
WHERE chatbots.id = user_domain_chatbots.chatbot_id AND user_domains.id = user_domain_chatbots.user_domain_id AND (EXISTS (SELECT 1 
FROM users 
WHERE user_domains.id = users.user_domain_id AND users.username = $4::VARCHAR)))) OR (EXISTS (SELECT 1 
FROM teams, team_chatbots 
WHERE chatbots.id = team_chatbots.chatbot_id AND teams.id = team_chatbots.team_id AND (EXISTS (SELECT 1 
FROM users, user_teams 
WHERE teams.id = user_teams.team_id AND users.id = user_teams.user_id AND users.username = $5::VARCHAR))))) 
 LIMIT $6::INTEGER

data.gen_ai.output.messages:
[{"role": "assistant", "parts": [{"type": "text", "content": "You provided the "Basic Calculator Requirements Document." Here’s a concise explanation and summary of its key points, organized for quick review.

Main purpose
- Define requirements to build a simple, reliable web-based calculator (desktop + mobile) that supports core arithmetic and a clean, accessible UI. This version focuses on foundational features; advanced functions are out of scope.

Scope and out-of-scope
- In scope: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, decimal support, responsive UI, error handling, accessibility, cross-browser/device compatibility, testing, and basic performance/security guarantees.
- Out of scope (for future releases): memory/scientific/statistical functions, history, parentheses, graphing, unit conversions, and advanced customization.

Functional requirements (what it must do)
- Basic operations: +, −, ×, ÷ with support for positive/negative operands, decimals, and proper formatting.
- Division-by-zero handling and error messages.
- Input rules: multi-digit entry, single decimal point, prevent invalid formats (e.g., multiple decimals, consecutive operators).
- Chaining operations supported (use previous result as next operand).
- Result formatting: thousands separators, scientific notation for very large numbers, reasonable decimal rounding and trimming trailing zeros.

User interface requirements (how it should look/behave)
- Primary display for input/results; optional secondary display for current operation.
- Numeric keypad (0–9), operation buttons (+, −, ×, ÷), control buttons (C, =, ., optional AC).
- Grid layout, consistent styling, visual feedback on press, readable fonts and responsive text.
- Touch-friendly targets (minimum ~44px), sufficient spacing to avoid accidental presses.

Non-functional requirements (quality attributes)
- Usability: intuitive familiar layout, minimal learning curve, keyboard navigation, screen-reader ARIA labels, high-contrast color scheme.
- Performance: instant calculations (<100 ms), app load <2 seconds, smooth responses under rapid use.
- Resource efficiency: small memory/CPU footprint and optimized for older devices.
- Error handling: clear messages, prevent invalid sequences, graceful overflow/underflow management, recoverable error states.
- Compatibility: modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge), backward compatibility for recent versions, responsive across desktop/tablet/mobile breakpoints.

Technical specifications & constraints
- Implement with HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript only (no frameworks required).
- Optional PWA features and local storage for preferences.
- Clean, modular, documented code with comprehensive error handling.

Testing, QA, deployment
- Unit tests for calculation logic, integration tests for UI, cross-browser and mobile testing.
- Performance testing (load times, stress with rapid presses, memory/battery impact).
- Code reviews required; target ≥90% test coverage for critical functions.
- Deployable to standard web hosting with minified assets and a checklist validating accessibility and performance.

Security & privacy
- No personal data collection; all calculations run locally in the browser; no external API calls.

Risks & mitigations
- Browser compatibility — mitigate via testing matrix and progressive enhancement.
- Floating-point precision — mitigate using precision handling libraries or custom logic to avoid JS decimal quirks.
- Performance on low-end devices — mitigate with profiling and optimization, graceful degradation.

Internationalization considerations
- Design to allow future localization: decimal/group separators differences, RTL support, UTF-8 encoding, and handling various keyboard/input methods.

Acceptance criteria
- All basic arithmetic works correctly, UI responsive and accessible, error handling as specified, cross-browser compatibility confirmed, performance targets met, and tests passing.

Development plan (high-level timeline)
- Phase 1 (Weeks 1–2): core logic, parsing/validation, error handling, tests.
- Phase 2 (Weeks 2–3): UI design and integration, responsive layout.
- Phase 3 (Weeks 3–4): testing, cross-browser checks, accessibility verification, performance tuning.

If you want, I can:
- Produce a one-page requirements checklist for developers/testers.
- Convert the functional requirements into user stories.
- Draft a minimal implementation plan with estimated tasks and hours. Which would you prefer?"}], "finish_reason": "stop"}]
data.gen_ai.provider.name: openai
